skill.name: meeting-assistant
skill.description: Extracts key meeting information in chronological order and outputs decisions and action items; use when you need meeting minutes, action tracking, or project sync notes from transcripts or raw notes.
skill.summary: Converts meeting transcripts into structured minutes with timeline summaries, decisions, and action items
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 46 static analysis findings are false positives. This is an instruction-only skill with no executable code, no network calls, no file system access, and no environment variable access. The static analyzer misinterpreted markdown backtick formatting as Ruby/shell command execution and natural language text as cryptographic algorithms. The skill is purely documentation that guides an AI model to structure meeting transcripts into organized minutes.

content.user_title: Transform meeting transcripts into structured minutes and action plans
content.value_statement: This skill converts raw meeting notes and transcripts into organized meeting minutes with timeline summaries, decisions, action items, and open issues. It saves time by producing consistent, structured outputs that are ready to share with your team.
actual_capabilities: Extracts a chronological timeline of discussion points from meeting transcripts
Identifies and separates confirmed decisions from items pending confirmation
Extracts action items with owner, deadline, and status tracking
Highlights disagreements and open issues requiring resolution
Masks sensitive information such as email addresses and phone numbers in outputs
Consolidates minutes from multiple meetings into a single progress report
limitations: Requires meeting transcripts or detailed notes with timestamps for best results
Cannot attend meetings or generate transcripts from audio directly
Accuracy depends on the quality and completeness of the input text
May misidentify decisions or action items in ambiguous or informal language

use_cases.title: Weekly Team Sync Documentation
use_cases.description: Transform raw notes from weekly standup meetings into structured minutes with action items and owners.

use_cases.title: Executive Meeting Decision Logging
use_cases.description: Capture board and steering committee decisions, separating confirmed resolutions from items that need further review.

use_cases.title: Client Meeting Follow-up
use_cases.description: Convert client call notes into a professional summary with clear next steps and assigned responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Meeting Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate meeting minutes from the following transcript: [paste transcript]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick summary of a short team meeting

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Minutes with Action Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract a chronological timeline, decisions, disagreements, and action items from this meeting transcript. For each action item include the task, owner, and deadline. [paste transcript]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive minutes for an important project sync

prompt_templates.title: Decision-Focused Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: From this meeting transcript, extract only the confirmed decisions and items that are still pending confirmation. Separate them clearly and note who made each decision. [paste transcript]

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Meeting Consolidation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have transcripts from three consecutive project status meetings. Consolidate them into a single progress report showing: timeline of key decisions made across meetings, action items by owner across all meetings, and topics that remain unresolved. [paste transcripts]
prompt_templates.scenario: Cross-meeting trend analysis for program managers

output_examples.input: Meeting: Sprint Retrospective. Date: 2025-02-10. Participants: Alice, Bob, Carol. [14:00] Alice: Completed the API integration. [14:05] Bob: Frontend testing is behind schedule. [14:10] Carol: Decision to extend sprint by 2 days.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a structured meeting minutes document with sections for Basic Information, Timeline Summary, Decisions, Action Items, and Open Issues. The timeline shows three key discussion points ordered by time. The decision section records the sprint extension. Action items are displayed in a table with Owner, Deadline, and Status columns.

output_examples.input: Project sync notes: Q1 goals on track. Risk: test environment not ready. Budget approved. Action: Alice to draft technical solution by Friday.
output_examples.output: The skill parses the unstructured notes and generates a timeline-ordered summary. It identifies the budget approval as a confirmed decision and extracts the action item assigned to Alice with a Friday deadline. Any missing information like deadlines or owners is marked as TBD or Unassigned.

best_practices: Include timestamps and speaker labels in your input for the most accurate chronological ordering
Review and edit the generated action items to ensure owners and deadlines are correct before sharing
Use consistent speaker names across meetings to enable cross-meeting consolidation and trend analysis
anti_patterns: Do not paste transcripts with personal data expecting automatic masking - review outputs for sensitive information
Do not expect the skill to infer missing action items or decisions that are not stated in the transcript
Do not use this skill for real-time meeting transcription - it requires pre-existing notes or transcripts

faq.question: Does this skill work with audio recordings of meetings?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires text transcripts or written notes. It cannot process audio files directly.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for real-time meeting assistance?
faq.answer: No. The skill operates on pre-existing transcripts and notes, not live meeting streams.

faq.question: What input format does the skill expect?
faq.answer: Any text format with timestamps and speaker labels works best. Plain paragraph notes also work but produce less precise timelines.

faq.question: Does the skill access external tools or services?
faq.answer: No. The skill is instruction-only and operates entirely within the conversation. It makes no network calls and accesses no local files.

faq.question: Can the skill track action items across multiple meetings?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide transcripts from multiple meetings and the skill will consolidate action items by owner and track status changes over time.

faq.question: How does the skill handle sensitive information?
faq.answer: The skill masks common identifiers such as email addresses and phone numbers in generated outputs. However, you should still review outputs before sharing.
